CTA Transit — Redesign Directions
5 directions · mobile + desktop for each. Drag to reorder. Click any label to focus.
Notes
Legend

5 ways to rethink the CTA routefinder.

Each row = one design direction. Mobile PWA first, then desktop split view. Inside a direction you'll see screens for the core journey: home, results, expanded route, live trip, and service alerts.
D1 Refined Dark — polish the current direction.D2 Editorial — printed-transit-map typography.D3 Playful — rounded, warm, Citymapper-y.D4 Departure Board — split-flap brutalism.D5 Line Color — CTA palette as the UI.
Decisions locked
· Hi-fi mockups
· Mobile + desktop
· Dark + editorial + playful + brutalist + color-forward
· Real-time data is the hero
· Route cards emphasize total time & transfers
· Stylized schematic map (not functional map)
· Custom palette, CTA-inspired
Shared · Schematic Map
Direction 1 — Refined Dark
Current dark theme, tightened up. High-density, hairlines, vibrant line-color pills.

Page 2 — Dispatches

Notices & Delays

Updated hourly from the CTA dispatch feed.
REMajor

Weekend track work

Single tracking, Howard to Belmont. Expect delays of 10 to 15 minutes.

REMinor

Jackson elevator out of service

Use Monroe or Roosevelt for step-free access until further notice.

BLAdvisory

New overnight schedule

From May 1: trains run every fifteen minutes between 1 and 4 AM.

MAJORRed LINEThis weekend
Howard → Belmont single track

INFOBlue LINEUpcoming

Trains every 15 minutes from 1 AM to 4 AM, seven days a week.
